resolution_rate_note: 100% resolution rate for all in-scope entities. The 3 out-of-scope items (ProBook and Enterprise Laptop Series, EdgeStation Workstations, SIEM connector aspect) are hardware platforms and third-party integrations outside Layer 1 scope and require no registry action. Initial phase produced 3 direct resolutions; the remediation phase completed 6 additional resolutions via 4 new entity registrations (OS-013, FW-013, DRV-009, DRV-010) and 2 alias additions (SEC-004 gained 'Security Agent'; MGT-010 gained 'Endpoint Management Agent' and 'Endpoint Mgmt Agent'). Note: BIOS/System BIOS variants (FW-001, FW-005) were already resolved in the initial phase; the 9 count reflects distinct resolved logical entity groups.

duplicate_mappings.description: Multiple raw variant names that resolved to or group under the same logical entity.

duplicate_groups.deduplication_method: canonical + alias + normalized match — all three variants map to same entity
semantic grouping — 'firmware' is a type-string normalization of null-name records representing same product. New entity FW-013 registered.
case normalization — lowercase variant is the same entity. New entity DRV-009 registered.
source evidence — RCAND-000395 explicitly equates both forms. Aliases added to MGT-010.

entities: Endpoint Management Agent via MGT-010 alias (post-addition)

resolution_method_summary.note: BIOS group succeeded on passes 1, 2, and 3 independently — counted once. Total distinct logical resolutions for in-scope entities = 10/10. Remediation actions: 4 new entity registrations + 2 alias additions.

candidate_coverage_analysis.note: All 42 candidates now have full entity resolution coverage. Every software component referenced in RCAND-000360 through RCAND-000401 maps to a registered RC2 entity. Hardware platform references (ProBook, EdgeStation) are handled as condition metadata scope — they do not require Layer 1 entity registration.

data_quality_findings_summary.issue: component_type mislabeling: 'System Firmware' labeled as component_type='bios' in RCAND-000364 and RCAND-000374
data_quality_findings_summary.impact: Resolved — FW-013 (System Firmware) is now registered. Phase 4 rule production should normalise component_type to 'firmware' for these two records when mapping to FW-013.

data_quality_findings_summary.issue: Null component_name populated with type-string by normalizer
data_quality_findings_summary.impact: Low — all now resolve correctly. Lowercased type-string forms ('bios'→FW-001, 'firmware'→FW-013, 'driver'→DRV-009) map to registered entities.

data_quality_findings_summary.issue: Readiness process artifacts ('Reboot Cycle', 'Step 1') extracted as component requirements
data_quality_findings_summary.impact: Low — discarded before resolution. Phase 4 should handle RCAND-000398 and RCAND-000401 with special readiness requirement logic rather than component entity resolution.

resolved_entities_ready_for_phase4: MGT-010 (Endpoint Agent / Endpoint Management Agent) — 5 candidates

phase4_readiness_assessment.recommended_action_before_phase4: None. All in-scope entities are resolved. Phase 4 rule production may proceed against the updated RC2 registry (v1.1.0-rc2, entity_count=60).
